skill.name: conversion-copywriting
skill.description: Create conversion-focused landing page, service page, email, ad, and proposal copy using customer voice, verified claims, and proven page structures.
skill.summary: Create conversion-focused marketing copy using customer voice, verified claims, and structured page sections.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown fences, ordinary prose, and field names in a documented output example. No executable commands, weak cryptography implementation, network reconnaissance behavior, exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown fences flagged as external commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown code fences, template outlines, banned phrase examples, jargon replacements, integration references, and a documented output example. They do not execute commands or instruct the agent to run shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are Markdown formatting and inline code references in documentation. No shell command, Ruby expression, subprocess call, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography substring match
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms at lines that contain the skill description, verification report wording, and a meta description field name. The file does not implement MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic routine.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose and a sample metadata field. There is no cryptographic API usage or security-sensitive hashing behavior to exploit.

low_findings.title: False positive: Network reconnaissance substring match
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported network reconnaissance at process-outline lines. These lines describe copywriting page sections and do not contain network tools, host discovery, port scanning, DNS lookup, or external requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines use the word Process as a marketing page section. No network reconnaissance command or network access instruction appears in context.

content.value_statement: Marketing pages often fail when they use generic claims and vague customer benefits. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft copy from customer voice, proof points, and verified claims.
actual_capabilities: Drafts homepage, service page, about page, email, ad, and proposal copy.
Uses VOC research, testimonials, and documented support language as source material.
Applies structured sections for heroes, proof, process, FAQs, and calls to action.
Replaces vague jargon with clearer language based on a banned phrase list.
Produces a verification summary for uniqueness, verified claims, and unresolved warnings.
limitations: Requires source material to produce accurate customer voice and proof points.
Cannot verify licenses, statistics, or testimonials unless the user provides evidence.
Does not perform an independent plagiarism scan outside the model workflow.
May need review for regulated industries such as legal, medical, and finance.

use_cases.title: Launch a Service Page
use_cases.description: Turn service details, proof points, and customer objections into a complete page draft.

use_cases.title: Refresh Website Messaging
use_cases.description: Rewrite generic page sections with clearer value propositions and customer language.

use_cases.title: Prepare Campaign Copy
use_cases.description: Create aligned email, ad, and proposal copy from the same verified messaging base.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with core business details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create landing page copy for [business]. Use these customer notes: [VOC]. Include hero, problem, benefits, proof, FAQ, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a Service Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve an existing page with stronger conversion structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this service page for clarity and conversion: [page copy]. Preserve verified claims, remove vague jargon, and add a concise verification summary.

prompt_templates.title: Build Copy from Research
prompt_templates.scenario: Use customer research and competitor insights together.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create homepage copy using this VOC research: [quotes], competitor insights: [notes], and proof points: [evidence]. Mark any unsupported claim.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Multi-Asset Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for consistent messaging across channels.
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a campaign for [offer]. Produce landing page sections, three email angles, five ad hooks, and a claim verification report from these sources: [materials].

output_examples.input: Homepage copy for a cleaning company with customer quotes, licenses, and service area details.
output_examples.output: A homepage draft with hero, problem, value propositions, proof, process, FAQ, CTA, and a verification summary.

output_examples.input: Existing service page that sounds generic and includes unsupported claims.
output_examples.output: A clearer service page rewrite with plain language, stronger proof, removed jargon, and flagged unsupported claims.

output_examples.input: VOC notes and competitor insights for a new local service campaign.
output_examples.output: A campaign messaging set with page sections, email angles, ad hooks, and source-backed claim notes.

best_practices: Provide real customer quotes, testimonials, credentials, and service details before drafting.
Ask the model to mark every unsupported claim instead of filling gaps with assumptions.
Review final copy for brand voice, legal requirements, and factual accuracy before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for customer voice when no customer research is available.
Do not publish claims about awards, licenses, or results without source evidence.
Do not use the output as legal, medical, or financial compliance review.

faq.question: What type of copy does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates landing page, service page, about page, email, ad, and proposal copy.

faq.question: Does it require customer research?
faq.answer: Customer research is strongly recommended because the skill depends on real customer language.

faq.question: Can it verify every claim automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can flag unsupported claims, but users must provide evidence for verification.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated industries?
faq.answer: It can draft copy for regulated industries, but expert compliance review is still required.

faq.question: Can it improve existing copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rewrite existing sections to reduce jargon and improve conversion structure.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
